title: Harmonia
description: First village entry. Lead-focused intro. The other four are present but unspoken — their introductions land in their respective spotlight chapters.

lines: Harmonia was the kind of village that existed to be left. A waypoint. A place you were from, not a place you stayed.
Six houses, a chapel, a shop that sold swords it had never tested, and an inn whose slime mascot had been repainted so many times it now resembled a vaguely threatening cloud.
The monsters were getting worse. The bats were bigger. The slimes were angrier. Traders stopped coming.

steps.text: So five people showed up. Not because they were brave. Not because they were special. Because they were there, and nobody else was.

fighter.text: The Fighter came because he had heard there was trouble, and trouble was what fighters were for.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: If there's trouble, I'll stand in front of it. That's... what I do.

cleric.text: The Cleric came because the chapel had been empty for a week and God, or whatever heard her prayers, had clearly decided someone should investigate.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: A calling is a calling. You don't second-guess it.

mage.text: The Mage came because the pattern he'd found in the monster attacks was too interesting to ignore.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The attack frequency follows a geometric progression. Someone is designing these encounters.

rogue.text: The Rogue had been sleeping under the inn's eaves. Elder Theron saw a figure there, and pointed. It had been that kind of morning.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: I was passing through. Made the critical error of still being here.

bard.text: The Bard came because the Bard went everywhere there was a story. Which was both her gift and everyone's curse.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: What a tale this shall be!

default.text: They had come for their own reasons, this one most of all.

steps.text: Four others had come too. Each for their own reasons. The Bard would tell you about them later, at length, with footnotes — but for now they kept their distance, waiting to be needed.
A notice had been nailed to the door of the Sleepy Slime Inn. It said to report to Elder Theron at the village square.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Then we should find him. It's the polite thing to do.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Elder Theron. Noted. The square is the most efficient meeting point.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Square's that way. Let's get this over with.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Elder Theron. Let's find him — he's supposed to be somewhere in the village.
